overall_assessment: Found 3 P0 bugs and 4 P1 issues. P0: (1) batch_status.py get_pending_by_source destroyed by indentation causing AttributeError. (2) transcript.py get_browser_cookies missing import causing NameError. (3) batch_status.py set_channel_metadata INSERT OR REPLACE destroys data on partial updates.

findings.title: batch_status.py: get_pending_by_source method destroyed by indentation error
findings.description: Lines 886-894 contain code for get_pending_by_source but lack a def statement. The code sits after the finally: conn.close() of upsert_channel at the same indentation. Python treats the docstring as a no-op then executes the SQL query and return unconditionally on every upsert_channel call. The class has no get_pending_by_source method so the public API at lines 1368-1372 raises AttributeError.

findings.title: transcript.py: get_browser_cookies called without import causing NameError
findings.description: Line 1192 calls get_browser_cookies(firefox) but the function is never imported. Defined in csf/youtube_auth.py but no import exists in transcript.py. Causes NameError when whisper fallback is triggered.

findings.title: batch_status.py: set_channel_metadata INSERT OR REPLACE destroys existing data on partial updates
findings.description: Uses INSERT OR REPLACE which deletes existing row then inserts new one with only provided fields. All params default to None. Partial updates destroy playlist_id video_count_estimate next_page_token quota_exhausted_at etc. Contrast with upsert_channel which correctly merges.

findings.title: transcript.py: _get_scheduler and _get_nlm_scraper singleton race without lock
findings.description: Lines 38-42 and 72-82 check/set module-level singletons without any lock. Under concurrent thread access multiple threads can create duplicate instances. Contrast with _get_auth_rate_limiter at lines 302-308 which uses double-checked locking.

findings.title: transcript.py: CookieFreshnessTracker.is_fresh() lock gap allows thundering herd
findings.description: Releases self._lock after TTL check runs subprocess probe (~30s) then re-acquires. Between release and re-acquire other threads with expired TTL also start probes causing multiple concurrent nlm login --check calls.

findings.title: source_enumerator.py: _api_request quota state mutations without locking
findings.description: Mutates _key_state dict (calls_made += 1 units_consumed += cost) without any locking. Under concurrent calls dict mutations interleave causing incorrect quota tracking. No threading.Lock in module.

findings.title: orchestrator.py: imports private _VIDEO_ID_PATTERN from csf.transcript
findings.description: Line 28 imports _VIDEO_ID_PATTERN (private by convention). If transcript.py refactors this symbol orchestrator.py breaks. source_enumerator.py defines its own copy at line 310 instead of importing.
